markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's official November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, set after delays from a May 2026 target and earlier 2025 plans, anchors trader focus on the roughly five-month window remaining. Anticipation centers on summer marketing ramps, including potential new trailers and Take-Two Interactive earnings updates that could signal any final polish or shifts. The game's cultural dominance as the largest entertainment launch in years compresses competition, with other studios avoiding the November slot, while GTA Online's ongoing support and pre-order leaks reinforce steady momentum. High-volume novelty outcomes in the market reflect how viral speculation thrives amid this fixed, high-stakes timeline.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 2026 hung jury on the Jessica Mann rape charge, have driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” at 82%. Prosecutors must decide by late June whether to pursue a fourth trial on that count, while Weinstein, now 74 and facing documented health decline, continues serving his separate 16-year California sentence. The stalled Manhattan cases, repeated jury deadlocks, and his age create significant barriers to additional convictions or extended incarceration, outweighing smaller probabilities for 20-30 years or shorter terms. The June 24 hearing remains the key near-term catalyst that could shift these market-implied odds.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights stands tied 2-2 after Carolina's 5-3 Game 4 victory on June 9, where Jordan Staal scored twice and Nikolaj Ehlers added three points in a comeback effort that overcame multiple lead changes. Both clubs advanced through strong postseason runs, with the Hurricanes dominating the Eastern Conference Finals and the Golden Knights sweeping the Western Conference Finals against Colorado. Carolina's recent form, including strong goaltending from Brandon Bussi and depth scoring, has positioned the team with a slight edge in trader consensus ahead of Game 5 at home. The Golden Knights have shown resilience through earlier series swings and contributions from players like Mitch Marner, keeping the matchup competitive in a best-of-seven format.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks' commanding 3-1 lead in the 2026 NBA Finals, secured by a franchise-record 29-point comeback victory in Game 4, has driven their 78.5% implied probability as champion. New York's regular-season balance, anchored by Jalen Brunson and a top-tier net rating, combined with series momentum and home-court resilience, underpins trader consensus. The San Antonio Spurs' 21% odds reflect their strong 62-20 regular season and Victor Wembanyama's dominance but have been tempered by the Game 4 collapse after leading by 29. With one Knicks win needed for the title and Game 5 looming, recent Finals developments heavily favor the current leader in this closely contested matchup.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit nearly even at the top of the 2026 World Cup winner market because both enter with elite squad depth, proven tournament experience, and strong recent form, including Spain’s Euro 2024 title. France’s narrow friendly loss to Ivory Coast on June 4 allowed Spain to edge ahead slightly in trader pricing, while Portugal has shortened on the back of Cristiano Ronaldo’s veteran leadership and a favorable group. England, Argentina, and Brazil remain tightly bunched behind due to star power and historical pedigree, though minor lengthening in recent days reflects the expanded 48-team field and the absence of any single dominant side. The bunched probabilities underscore how roster health, tactical fit, and early group-stage results will quickly separate contenders in a wide-open field.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party and state apparatus through ongoing military purges, institutional reforms, and centralized decision-making, with no verified signs of imminent removal or succession by 2027. Recent developments include his active role in launching the 15th Five-Year Plan for 2026-2030, New Year addresses projecting continuity, and diplomatic engagements, while elite analyses note that purges of senior PLA figures have reinforced rather than eroded his authority ahead of the 21st Party Congress. Occasional unconfirmed health or power-shift rumors have failed to alter official discourse or leadership visibility, aligning with trader consensus that barriers to early exit—such as the absence of designated successors and Xi's dominance over key institutions—remain substantial through late 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23% amid a fragmented field, driven by his national profile as California governor, fundraising strength, and visible role opposing the current administration through media appearances and policy clashes. Recent activity includes his memoir release and high-profile interviews positioning him ahead of the 2026 midterms, when Democrats aim to regain House control. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris follow in single digits, reflecting progressive mobilization and prior national exposure, while senators like Jon Ossoff and governors such as Josh Shapiro draw support from legislative records and swing-state appeal. The absence of formal announcements leaves room for consolidation around candidates demonstrating broad fundraising networks or primary turnout edges before 2028 primaries.
